title: Conjecture 1.1 (Friedgut, Kalai and Kindler)
statement_text: For every $\alpha \in (0, 1/2)$ there exists $k$ and $\tau > 0$ such that if $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with maximum independent set of size $\alpha n$, then there exist pairwise disjoint subsets of vertices $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_r$ in $G$ such that (1) $|A_i| = k$ for all $i$, (2) $|\bigcup_{i=1}^r A_i| \geq \tau n$, (3) every maximum independent set in $G$ intersects every set $A_i$.
context_text: This is Conjecture 3.1 of Friedgut, Kalai and Kindler (2021), motivated by questions about Hamming powers of Kneser graphs. The paper shows it is false for every fixed $\alpha \in (0, 1/2)$ even if requirement (2) is omitted, via an explicit infinite family of graphs; on the other hand, the assertion does hold for any $\alpha > 1/2$ by an old result of Hajnal.
paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture for every fixed $\alpha \in (0,1/2)$: constructs an infinite family of graphs $G_n$ with $n$ vertices, independence number at least $n/4$, and no set of fewer than $\sqrt{n}/2$ vertices intersecting all maximum independent sets.

title: Conjecture 1.2 (Bollobás, Erdős and Tuza)
statement_text: For any positive $\alpha$, if the size $\alpha(G)$ of a maximum independent set in an $n$-vertex graph $G$ is at least $\alpha n$, then $h(G) = o(n)$.
context_text: Raised by Bollobás, Erdős and Tuza (see [7] p. 224 or [5] p. 52), where $h(G)$ is the minimum cardinality of a vertex set intersecting every maximum independent set of $G$. The conjecture remains open for $n$-vertex graphs with independence number at most $n/2$ and for regular graphs with independence number at most $n/4$, and is noted to be significantly harder for graphs with independence number $\beta n$ when $\beta$ is a small positive constant.
paper_contribution: The constructions of Theorems 1.3 and 1.4 show $h(G)$ can be $\Theta(\sqrt{n})$ and $\Theta(t^2)$ respectively while $\alpha(G) > n/4$, consistent with the conjecture; the conjecture itself remains open in the stated ranges.
notes: Originally formulated for cliques; the paper uses the equivalent independent-set formulation via complementation.

title: Conjecture 1.6 (Friedgut, Kalai and Kindler)
statement_text: For any $\alpha \in (0, 1/2)$ there is an $\varepsilon = \varepsilon(\alpha) > 0$ so that for every graph $G$ with $n$ vertices and independence number $\alpha n$, $\alpha'(G) \leq \alpha - \varepsilon(\alpha)$.
context_text: This is Conjecture 2.9 of Friedgut, Kalai and Kindler (2021), where $\alpha'(G)$ denotes the expected independence number of the induced subgraph of $G$ on a uniform random subset of vertices, divided by $n$. It remains open for $n$-vertex graphs with independence number at most $n/4$ and for regular graphs with independence number at most $n/8$, and is noted to be significantly harder for small $\alpha$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all $\alpha > 1/4$: if $\alpha(G) = (1/4+\varepsilon)n$ then $\alpha'(G) \leq 1/4 + \varepsilon - \varepsilon^2/3$; also proves it for regular graphs whenever $\alpha > 1/8$.

title: Informal Conjecture on Chromatic Number of $G_{m,t}$
statement_text: The chromatic number of the graph $G_{m,t}$ described in the proof of Theorem 1.4, where $4t^2 \leq m$, is $\Theta(t^2)$.
context_text: Alon states this as a conjecture he raised more than ten years ago, motivated by results in [2] (Alon, Hassidim, Lubetzky, Stav and Weinstein, FOCS 2008) and mentioned in several lectures. The paper establishes that the chromatic number is at least $2t$ and at most $O(t^2)$, leaving the exact order unresolved.
notes: Stated informally in the Remarks section (Section 3) without a labelled theorem environment; attributed to the author himself from more than ten years prior but cited only via lectures, not a prior publication.
